author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
# Findings
Reproduced drift on a fresh colocated fixture. `jjf init` alone moves
`.git/HEAD` to `refs/jj/root` because the 4-CLI dance ends with
`jj new root()`, putting `@` on an unbranched commit; jj rewrites
`.git/HEAD` to mirror. Universal across mutating verbs. Reads
(`show`, `ls`) and `remote add|ls|rm` do NOT drift.
`--ignore-working-copy` does NOT prevent it — verified directly.
`jj edit main` recovers `@` but leaves `.git/HEAD` on `refs/jj/root`;
the documented recovery was incomplete. Full recovery is
`git symbolic-ref HEAD refs/heads/main && git reset --hard main`.

# Recommendation
Option A + D. Preflight `refuse_self_hosted_write` detects the
jjforge source repo via marker combo (crates/jjf/Cargo.toml +
docs/storage-format.md), refuses mutating verbs (exit 2, kind
`self_hosted_write_refused`). `JJF_ALLOW_SELF_HOST=1` bypasses
loudly (text mode only — silent under --json for clean envelopes).
Reads pass through. CLAUDE.md rewritten to recommend the
sibling-workspace pattern with the correct recovery recipe.
Landed in e8760b0.

# Confidence
High on diagnosis and fix. Drift reproduction deterministic;
guard exercised by 7 new integration tests covering refusal,
JSON envelope, both bypass paths, read-verb passthrough, marker
walk-up, and false-positive absence. All 185 workspace tests
green.

# Open follow-ups
- Option B (full workspace isolation) still on the table as a
  separate ticket if the bypass-driven workflow proves friction-heavy.
- A single `jjforge.toml` marker would beat the two-file heuristic
  on robustness; defer until false-positives appear.
- Test fixture location for the "unrelated repo" case uses
  `std::env::temp_dir()` because tests/.scratch/ is itself inside
  the source tree. Cross-platform CI may need attention.
- Old CLAUDE.md recovery recipe ("jj edit main") was empirically
  wrong; anyone who memorized it should re-read the rewritten section.
